title: Diagnostics, Build Reports, Metrics, Profiles, and Failure Triage · Contract Workshop
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds bounded actionable build and publication evidence report from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded actionable build and publication evidence report while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.
Sort deterministically, deduplicate, cap cascades, promote configured warnings, summarize omissions, and return status from policy.
Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Diagnostic Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 2282 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Diagnostic Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent severity, stable code, source span, stage, message, safe context, cause, suggestion, and ownership separately. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Diagnostic Model, keep Diagnostic Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-redaction-retention-17j201
# Competency: ssg-redaction-retention.
# Case 6854: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_redaction_retention_17j201(diagnostics: int = 7, located: int = 7, build_stages: int = 10, measured_stages: int = 10, unredacted_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17j201 = "17j201-102j71-6854"
    assert evidence_variant_17j201
    evidence_axes_17j201 = (
        "reader17j201",
        "source6854",
        "parse102j71",
        "route17j2016854",
        "artifact685417j201",
        "recovery102j716854",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17j201)) == 6
    if diagnostics < 0 or located < 0:
        return False, located, "diagnostic-count"
    if located != diagnostics:
        return False, located, "location-coverage"
    if build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages:
        return False, measured_stages, "metric-coverage"
    if unredacted_secrets > 0:
        return False, unredacted_secrets, "diagnostic-leak"
    assert located == diagnostics
    return True, measured_stages, "actionable-build-report"
